plan_metadata.name: Feature Simple Bot With Symbol Link Target js playground

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" popup within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Other Teams Capabilities" dropdown option in the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selector within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Select the "Simple Bot" option from the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension window in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown located in the Workspace Folder selection box to specify the project root directory.
Click the "New Folder" button in the VS Code Explorer panel, located at the top-left toolbar of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type 'devTools/playground' into the new folder input field in the Explorer pane of Visual Studio Code, within the project.
Press Enter to confirm renaming the folder "devTools/playground" in the VS Code Explorer sidebar.
Use keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in the Visual Studio Code Explorer interface to open the Command Palette, with the workspace folder and the "devTools/playground" directory selected.
Type text: 'select and start debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate the debugging process.
Click the "Debug in Microsoft 365 Agents Playground" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette after searching for "debug".
Click on the "Type a message..." input box in the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground chat interface to activate the text entry field.
Type text "Hi" into the chat input box at the bottom of the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web interface.
Press the Enter key in the message input box at the bottom of the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web application to submit the typed message "Hi".
@assertion there is the reply "you said: Hi".
Click the browser tab close button (X) at the top-right corner of the Chrome window in the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground web application interface.
Click the "devTools" folder in the Explorer panel on the left side of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "playground" folder in the Explorer panel within Visual Studio Code, under the "devTools" directory.
@assertion there are files under "devTools/playground" folder.
